sentences.text: That was nice.
So fun.
Like, see faces, and….
Yes.
Remember, people are not just on Zoom, so….
Right.
How's your summer been?
Summer has been good.
A lot of pool, a lot of being outside, so it's been a good time.
Yeah, it's… it's, like, absolutely crazy that it's almost over already, but….

Oh my gosh, I know, like, everybody here took all their… back to school today, so… I can't believe… which, that doesn't mean anything to me yet, but, like, whenever school does start, I'm like, oh my gosh, like, it's gonna be, like, Halloween soon, and Thanksgiving, and then Christmas.
I feel like as soon as school starts, everything just kind of….
flies by.
It does.
And I feel like… I don't… I feel like school has been starting, like, consistently since, like, the middle of July for people.
I don't know.
Like, how or why, but….
It is interesting, like, everyone in Oklahoma starts in August.
Like, middle of August, yeah.
When do they get out, normally, like… May?
End of May, yeah.
Wow.
Yeah, I was always, like, an after-Labor Day kid.
I don't know if that's just, like, a Wisconsin thing or not, but….
Yeah, it must be.
That's interesting.
Those kids in Indiana started August 6th.
That's way too early.
Yeah.
That is early.
Jordan, did you do any fun summer travel?
Anywhere cool?
Yeah, we did, we went to Cabo.
We had a couple lake trips… Went to Scottsdale, stuff like that.
I played in, like, a golf tournament last weekend.
Got my butt pegged by, like, a lot of women 65 plus, so that's cool.
They were so good, I was like, oh my gosh.
That's all they do.
No, I know.
Lovely.
you need me to join our association.
I was like, I work.
They play, like, every day.
Like, actually, I'm working right now.
And they're so scary.
Like, I don't know why, you can just tell, you can see the ringleaders, it seems a little clicky.
I don't know, it was a little… it was a little intimidating.
Yeah.
How'd you play?
bad.
So much.
Oh!
I know.
I went with some friends.
Oh, well.
I went with some friends this summer, to a nice golf course in Wisconsin, and I was the worst player, so….
I hate that feeling.
I knew I was gonna be the worst, I was just, I was okay with it, you know?
Yeah, and thankfully, like, nobody cares, like, people only care about their own score and how they play, so I was like, you know what, whatever.
Is golf a new thing for you?
Are you trying to get into it?
What'd you say?
Sorry, is golf a new thing for you?
Like, a newer passion, or have you.
Yeah, it's… it's new-ish.
Like, I started summer of 22.
Oh, yeah, nice.
So, yeah, and everybody played, like, in high school, and one of the girls I was paired up with, she was like, yeah, I played, like, 10 years in the LPGA.
I was like, oh, wow.

So they had to, like, break down… because it was, the club championship of our club, so they were, you know, teaching me all the rules, because I'm like, I didn't play in high school or college, like, all these rules are very new to me, so… I mean, they were all nice, and kind of broke it down for me, as far as the ruling went.
LPGA, that's a flex.
I know.
I was like, what the heck?
Why am I paired up with these people?
Right, I would have been like, how did I stumble into this situation?
Hold on a second.
Can I change groups?
Yeah.
What happened here?
Exciting, the portfolio's growing, though, sounds like.
Yes!
produce?
It is, yeah, we're opening, like, our fifth property in Kansas City, so that's exciting.
We just had a grand opening here in Owasso, and then, we should have another one opening in Oklahoma, like, end of the year, or maybe, like, January or February, so… Thanks.
It's been good.
Wait, new lease-ups?
New lease-ups or acquisitions?
Yeah, new lease ups.
Yeah, cool, that's exciting.
Yeah, so it's been good.
I get busy.
Keep it on your toes.
Yeah.
Right?
I know, and then we have our, … a lot of planning has been going into our company conference, and that's when, you know, we fly in everybody.
From all of our markets, so it's a big thing in September, so we have that coming up, too.
And that's at the lake, so it's always fun.
Are you guys coming to that?
Actually, I haven't looked at the list.
I don't believe so, but I'd have to double-check the sponsorship side of things, but….
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I wasn't sure.
I didn't know if you guys, like, wanted any information, or… I mean, it probably would have been done, like, a couple months ago, but….
Yeah.
Yeah.
September.
Well, that's in 2 weeks.
Crazy.
I know.
Crazy.
So crazy.
Awesome.
Well, appreciate you jumping on.
Wanted to touch base with you first and foremost about the current properties that we have on the Google product of ours.
I know we checked in with you, like.
when was that, like, a month or two ago?

Last, and we showed you the new amenities feed, which we rolled out across your property, so I wanted to just make sure things are still going smoothly there, and just kind of a quick partnership overview, discover, you know, like.
how things are going, make sure things are going smoothly, run through some quick metrics so far that we've seen, and then Kylie has some new… New fun things to chat through.
Anything we're missing there that you wanted to make sure we ran through?
Nope, that looks good to me.
Cool.
Awesome.
Well, I think we kind of already touched on it.
Sounds like some big initiatives happening right now for you and the team is… new properties.
Is there anything else, like, any initiatives or, like, marketing projects that you're focusing on outside of the new properties, or are those kind of just, like, full steam ahead?
No, Full Steam Ahead, yeah, Full Steam Ahead.
Awesome.
What's the… for those new properties, like, what's your marketing strategy look like for those?
Ugh, that would….
varying.
be, yes, and more of, like, a Brittany question, if I had her on here, or Crystal.
Okay, yeah, that's no problem.
Just curious as to, like, what… What that all entails when you're… when you're looking at new properties and….
That marketing strategy looks like overall.
Overall, happy with the partnership, everything with Google, and everything we've got in motion there.
Yes.
Awesome.
Quickly run through, we've got 67 properties on.
I think we ended up taking one off, just because of some verification.
issues that we ran into early on.

Again, just a quick recap, you're on our Google AI product with our Maven suite, and then our contract did start in March, so we're just about halfway, almost, with the contract, so… I know previously we talked about this on the last check-in, you were working through some verifications on other properties, so whenever…
That's in motion, or if you have any questions, or anything else additionally there, we're always happy to help as well.
Okay, perfect.
Kylie, anything to add here?
Yeah, Jordan, have you gotten any updates on those properties that weren't verified?
I think there were, like, 15 or 20 properties that Brittany was still working on.
Yeah, I actually believe we're down to, like, maybe one or two, but a majority of them have been verified, yes.
Okay.
Cool, maybe I'll connect with Brittany then and see if she wants… like, if you guys want to get those properties added as well.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Perfect.
Always include just some, like, quick screenshots.

I don't… I'm not gonna, like, run through everything in full detail, because you're very aware of what we're doing here, but, screenshot here, of course, of that availability with your pricing availability at this property, and then that amenities feed now that we rolled out, so….

I'm getting a lot of feedback from customers that they're really liking having the amenities right with their pricing availability, just because they can, like, fully shop the property right in the products feed on Google, which is really great, so….
Yeah, I love that feature.
Really nice.
I can't… did we roll that out on all of… I know we maybe chose, like, a couple, but we ended up rolling it out everywhere.
Yep, yeah, I think we piloted a handful, and then once you guys, like, took a look at it, poked around, we rolled it out across the portfolio that we have on, so….
Awesome.
Yeah, I think that amenities feed really adds a lot to the profile, so excited that we have that up and running.
We have those daily posts again, amenity-based, those come through every day, information comes from your property management software.
And then lastly, our Q&A optimization, which again gets updated daily and just helps enhance and get those keywords on your profiles.
I know we're gonna talk to Brittany, Jordan, but with those properties now being verified, I'd highly recommend turning these features on.

I'm assuming there's not much content on those profiles, and it's just a matter of syncing the feed and getting Google access, and you'll have all of the features you have right here, the amenities feed, your pricing, everything, within a couple days.
So, it's not hard to do, so for… since they're verified, I definitely recommend it.
Okay, great.
Perfect.
Any questions on the features we have live?
No.
Okay, cool.
I figured, but just wanted to make sure we asked.
And then, I wanted to include a quick snippet here on just reporting we have since going live in March, to date, year over year.
So again, we're seeing those KPIs trending upward, which is great.

We've kind of hit that sweet spot right now in how long properties have been active for, so… Increasing 20% in website actions, like, it's kind of always the first KPI that I look at, but… Also included, which I don't think is a big surprise here, a quick… impression by device report, which I think is.
It's just kind of….
insane to look at.

Obviously, mobile is leading the charge across, usage, but I want to just But so far, we're seeing great results as far as those main KPIs that we're tracking on your Google Business Profile, which, like Patrick said, I think shows the impact that the product is making.
Yeah, that's awesome.
Those are great numbers.
Awesome.
Any questions on that?
And again, we can send this over.
In a follow-up, so that you have everything you need.
Yeah, that'd be perfect, no questions on this.
Looks good.
Cool.
Kylie?
Yeah, so, obviously the point of the whole Google product is to save your team time, to automate the process of posting to the profiles.
So we have the same, the same tech for social.
So remind me, what are you guys using for posting to your social properties right now?
Your profiles?
Dang, I wish we knew this, because right now we use Sochi, and we actually are moving everyone over to GTMA.
Patrick, are you familiar with GTMA?
I am, an agency in, based in Texas.
Yes?
I don't know why I'm thinking Atlanta, but I think maybe Austin, yeah.
I think they're gonna watch them.
Yeah.
Are they posted for you?
Oh, go ahead, Jordan.
Yes, yeah, they do.
So, I'm not sure if Crystal has signed that contract.
I believe she maybe has or is reviewing it.
But yeah, we're actually moving all of our socials over from Sochi over to them.
Do you even know what kind of drove that away from social?
What was the main problem with them?
We just wanted a refresh.

Like, we had been with Sochi for so long, and now they have all these huge agencies, we didn't really feel like it was, like, multifamily specific, and we were kind of just wanting something, something new, and a refresh.
You're not alone, Jordan.
We've heard, I think, I think you said the keywords, multifamily specific, and I think GTMA is multifamily specific.
They are.
Yeah, they actually mentioned that they've had the same, you know, they're probably hearing the same things that you guys are.
Totally.
That people are moving from Sochi for that reason.
Yeah, they're so focused on other industries now.
I mean, they're doing.
I like something.
Like, Chase Bank, you know?
I know, like, how do you compete with that?
Just so….
I get it.
It's different.
It's so different, like, the approach there in multifamily is, like.
You know, the product is units, and where people live, and you want to be… Everybody, you know….
thoughtful with your social approach, so… Right.
Any idea what GTMA entails?
I know them loosely, I know them more on, like, the paid side, I don't know as much on the organic side of what they're doing.
Yeah, we… honestly, we really liked their, platform, like, it was easy-to-use dashboard, and we're doing, like.
I want to say it's 75% white glove to where they'll be doing the posting, but we still have our option to go in and post ourselves.
I know it's, like, integrated with Canva, so that all of our properties can still use their Canva.
But yeah, they'll be handling, like, Instagram, Facebook.
Posting for us.
Okay.
Well, we can just give you, like, a… A lightweight run-through of what we're doing.
Maybe there's a fit where they both work together, maybe she hasn't signed the contract.
I mean, obviously, I know there's probably a lot of thought that went into moving from Sochi and looking at GTMA.
Right.
Play to the party here, but at least want to show you how we're thinking about it.
Okay.
In the background.
So Kylie's right, you know, Google Business is our flagship product, and it was launched because Google's important, but it's really hard to manually update Google.
And so a lot of our customers said, hey, we have that same problem with social.

You know, it's manual, it's… sometimes it gets… you know, left to the wayside, and we're not actually posting, it's not consistent, and so everything you see here is completely automated, and then we've layered on the ability to manually post as well.
So, we'll give you a lightweight run-through here, and then if it makes sense, we can connect with the rest of your team, and if it doesn't make sense, that's fine too.
Okay.
So we have a lot of our customers that are on our Google Business tool have added this, almost like an extension to what we're doing on Google.
So it's a very similar concept to the posts on Google, where it's property-specific, amenity-based posts.
So that's when… when the last time we talked about this, that was really all the posts were.
So, I wanted to bring this up again, because since then, we've added in new post types.

We've got a lot of feedback from customers that it's great to have the amenity-based posts, it's great to call out the pool, it's great to call out stainless steel appliances, but that they wanted the social content to be a little more… fit for both their current tenants and prospects.
So, Paige, if you want to go to the slide 13… So, we have some new post types we've added in since then.
So, those would be holiday posts, lifestyle posts, tour scheduling posts, and then we added a post that calls out your pet policy as well.
Oh, nice.
It's not only the property-specific, but it's all of these post types as well.
Okay, perfect.
So we've had customers switch from tools like Sochi that aren't industry-specific to us, obviously, for that reason.
It's a little bit different than the agency posts.
really based to automate all of it, to automate the whole process.
Right.
So, we do… the standard way we have it set up is two posts a week.
So, Tuesday and Thursday, you're getting a different post, so that's 8-ish posts a month, and it's gonna be one of each of these post types.
Okay, perfect.

And I… I do know one thing that GTMA did that we really liked is they… Would look at events, like, going around… going on around or in the market that the property was at, as well as, like, bakeries or restaurants or bars or whatever around the area.
And they would pull a photo from that, but somehow tie it back to the community.
So that would be more like, like, localized posts.
Yes.
That is something that we are testing right now as well.
We have some customers that we're doing it on, where we're pulling in, like, local restaurants, local cafes, local parks, to kind of verify the… give different post types.
So that is something that.
Yeah, I like that.
Yeah.
Okay, awesome.
in an automated fashion, I think in the future, … future, we would have our customers give us a list.

Maybe you're like, hey, here's 20 businesses that we'd like to highlight, but for now, it's, pulling in… because we know the location of your property is pulling in Restaurants, bakeries, parks, museums, and creating content based on that in an automated fashion.
There's some guardrails around, like, star reviews.
Like, we wouldn't pull and highlight anything that has less than 4.
5 stars, so we don't want to, like, have the AI create Random posts about businesses that are not reputable, so….
Right.
But yeah, the idea here is to… and we have a dashboard as well that you could manually post through.
and visibly see any of the… if you want to show that here, Kylie, give you some time to pull it up, but visibly see upcoming posts as well.

But the idea here is it's… We get your property-specific information, we'll create posts that are specific to your properties, we'll layer in more types of posts, like holidays, pet policies, schedule a tour, and then also here.
So this… better than me talking, we can just kind of walk through what it looks like here.
Yeah, here you go.
So… so this dash shows you all of the automated posts that are coming in, like, a schedule.
So you can see this is both Google posts and the, social posts.
So you can see all upcoming posts that have been created from the automated tool that we have for the next, like, 30 days out.
But then we also have this button that Patrick was just talking about.
So within this, you can click which property or properties you want these to go to, click which source you want it to go to, write your content, and then drop and drag either photos or videos.
So you could schedule that out, you could post those right now, or you can schedule them out.
Got it.

So if you wanted to look at the automated posts, and then decide which kind of content you wanted to layer in, whether it's, like, highlighting a pet or a maintenance person, different content that you would more manually add, you can do that all right in here.
Okay, very cool.
What are your thoughts on this… on this tool?
Sounds like you're getting closer with GTMA already.
Are you still open to looking at other options?
It honestly would just depend how far Crystal is in the process on her end.
Yeah.
so I would have to have a discussion with her.
I know that we… we really liked… Their platform, and we were really set on it, so… the likelihood of switching over is probably low, but also, I could still have a conversation with her.
Okay.
Yeah, would love to show her, and Brittany if she's interested as well.
Yeah, maybe jump on a call with Crystal for 10 minutes and just show her what's new with this, what this, dashboard looks like, and see what her thoughts are.
Yeah, perfect.
I'll get with her today, because I was already kind of gonna fill her in on the things we discussed today, so yeah, I'll get with her, and … Yeah, can get back with you.
Awesome.
To put you in, like, a frame of price mind, it's between $100 and $125, is where we land, per month, per property.
Okay, perfect.
Well, agency is typically more expensive than that, but you're getting maybe more of a hands-on, curated approach.
This is an automated play with some human interaction to create your own posts, so….
Got it.
Okay.
Between 100 and 125?

Yeah, like we did with Google, it depends on how many properties you bring on, to this, so… I don't know if you plan on all of them on GTMA, maybe that's something where you do a group there and a group with us, but it'll all depend on how many that you want to roll out, but just to put you in a frame of reference, it's not hundreds of dollars, it's between.
Hi.
Yeah.
Okay.
Perfect, thank you for that info.
And then for those 20 other Google properties, if you do decide to bring those on, I think, what are we at, Kyla?
It'd be the same price, right?
What are we at?
Is it 105?
Yep, mono.
That would stay the same.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah.
Alright.
Sounds good.
Thanks for hopping on.
We'll send over a follow-up email with the deck that we went over, and then, if Crystal's open to chatting through social a little bit, let us know.
Okay, sounds good, thank you guys.
Awesome.
Thanks, George.
Good luck with the golf game, keep it going.
Have a good rest of your day.
Have a good rest of your day and weekend.
Bye.
Thank you, George.
Bye.
Maybe you need some, like, hot tea for your… I know.
That would be good, I feel.
I feel like that would help.
Hello?
Hello?
Hello!
How are you?
Good, how are you?
Good!
We had our company off-site this week, and we ended with karaoke, and my voice is non-existent, so….
Kylie's in, like, half of everybody else, so it's… it's common right now.
I was… I didn't even sing, like, I did not get on the stage.
I was just… Like, cheering you.
go on.
I thought I was a backup singer the entire night or something.
Oh, that's funny.
That sounds like a fun company event.
It was, yeah.
We all got together in Chicago for a couple days.
It was really nice.
I feel like everyone was excited, like, was like, oh, karaoke, okay, I don't know.
Like, you know, like, I think you could, like, psych yourself out a little bit in your head, and then we all got to the karaoke place, and we were like, everyone was, like, loving their life.
I'm like, this was… this was actually a good choice, despite everyone kind of being like, I don't know, is this… are we gonna like this?
Everyone….
Yeah, it gets everybody out of their comfort zone for a bit.
Yes.
Well, how are you guys?
What's new with you?
Oh, we've been moving and shaking.
I'm sorry I'm not on camera.
I'm actually eating my breakfast as we speak.
I've been on call since 8, and I'm very behind this morning on getting ready.
No worries at all.
Never required to turn cameras on for us.
This is….
Nope.
Well, thank you guys for jumping on.
I'm happy that we're able to do this.

I know it's been a little bit since we started getting properties on, so we're kind of going… planning to use this as, like, a kickoff call, and maybe, like, our first check-in call, since we do have a handful of properties on with you all.
Sure, sounds great.
We were also thinking, kind of maybe run through, like, a high-level demo, if that's okay with you guys.

I know some of you've seen a demo, I know, Christina, you were able to connect with Patrick and see a demo, but we're thinking, like, go through use cases of this and how customers are thinking about this, how others around Cushman are thinking about this and positioning this.
Go over a quick demo, and then a couple, like, new features that we… you guys have access to right now with the package that we don't have on your properties yet.
How does that sound?
Is there anything else that you guys wanted to add into, like, the agenda for the day?
Yeah, Taylor, or, Kaylee, or Kylie, if you don't mind, going over the MAC, properties, the ones that we just transitioned and giving status updates.

I know Taylor King, has been… she came in… she's new to CW, but now she's overseeing this portfolio, so I just kind of want to… Touch base and see where we're at, and if there's anything that you need from us, make sure that these go live.
Yep, yeah, we definitely did want to talk about that as well, because we have not received the Google Access or the feed syndications yet.
So it looks like… I had… Dean… Dean is your implementation specialist.
Looks like he reached out on Monday.
Yeah, I use access, through Rent Cafe for the feeds.
Okay.
it's rentable, right?
Which is… yeah, so I gave… I did that earlier this week, ….
And then, you said you needed Google Analytics access?
Just Google access, like, management access to the profile.
Okay.
Okay.
I will make note to do that as well.
I'm gonna select Dean right now really quickly, just in case he has any other updates over the last couple hours.
So with this feed, it's just those two steps for implementation.
The feed turning on on your end sometimes can take, like, 24 hours for us to receive it on our side.
Yeah, you should have already received it, because I believe I did it on either Monday or Tuesday.
Okay.
Alright, I just asked.
Did you want me to slack him, or did you?
I just did.
Perfect.
Yeah, I just added him… But yeah, when he messages me back, I'll let you know if there's been any updates there.
Okay, sounds good, thank you.
No, he just got back to me.
Okay, he said….
I said, yep.
We just received them this morning should be live by tomorrow, if there's no additional delays.
Awesome.
Wonderful.
Cool.
Yeah, so we'll send you a status update as soon as those are live.
Okay, so it's just the Google Business Profile access now is what you need.
No, he says he has that now, too.
Oh, yeah.
Yep.
Wonderful.
Okay.
Cool.
Good, we'll get that one out of the way.
Okay, great.
Anything else that you wanted to chat through before I jump in?
No, I didn't have anything unless Desiree or Christina did.
No, I don't.
Nope, I'm just looking forward to seeing how we can optimize our, active profiles.
Yeah, awesome.
Cool.
Well, let me jump in here.
So, really the point behind the product is automation.
So, automating to the Google business profiles to both save teams time from manual posting, but also increase exposure for your properties, fully optimize them, so it's little to no lift on your end.
So, not news for you guys, that's why you've signed up some properties.
But all of this is powered by your feeds.
So, that's, like, the source of truth for all of the information we're pulling in, is the feeds.

During implementation, our team is also looking at the property websites, so when you add those MAC properties, we're pulling directly from the feed, but then we'll go in and we'll look and see if there's any additional photography, or if there's any, amenities that we're seeing listed on the website that aren't in the feed.
We'll go ahead and we'll take those as well.
So, just so you're aware of where this content's coming from, and with… it's Rent Cafe that most of your properties are on?
Is that right?
Yes.
So, with Rent Cafe, we can ping 4 times a day.

So, 4 times a day, we can go in, that's most relevant for the pricing feed, but 4 times a day, we can go in and pull what you have available right now, what pricing is looking like, to keep it as updated as possible.
And then the reasons behind, the product is Google tells us that the Google business profile itself has the highest influence overall on your local SEO.
And then we also know that with the AI overviews, Google is actually using the content that lives on the profile as one of the sources for answering those AI overview questions.
So, that just speaks to the importance of why we're pushing amenity-based content so often, as well as having the pricing and availability for people that are searching that way.
That's why it's important to have a fully filled-out profile, so Google can use that content.
Any questions on any of this so far?
Not for me.
Nope, not from me.
Correct.

Then, jumping into… this is just talking about the AI overviews, and how year over year, we continue to see those, used more often, and people are really using that as oftentimes, the first step when they're Googling something, they'll look at the AI overview before scrolling further.
And then jumping into the product itself.
So, the high level is we're using both the syndication with the feed, and then our internal Build AI tool to create this content.
And the areas throughout the profile that we're doing that is the daily posts.
So every day, we're using the photography and the amenity-based information from the feed, coupled with the AI tool we have to write the content.
And we make sure that the content that we're writing is unique every day.

We're obviously gonna have to cycle through photography at some point, but we want the content to be unique, because that's something that Google looks at, is they look at the uniqueness of your content, the frequency of how often you're posting, and then they really want the content on your profiles to be Specific to the business, so your property.
So, all of this is built to fit those criteria that Google's looking for.
The next spot is the products feed.
So, that's the pricing and availability feed, again, updated 4 times a day with Rent Cafe.
concession data.
We're able to pull concessions from your feed to your profile, and that comes up in the posts, so I can show you what that looks like as well.
And then the Q&A.
So, automating the Q&A, we've had a lot of customers, even, especially this year, tell us that they're really looking at adding FAQs to their property websites for the AI overview reasons.
We know that Google really likes FAQ type of content.
So this is the same thing, just on your Google profiles.
So we're posting and answering questions to Q&A, all automated.
I have a question for the Q&A.
If there was, say, like, a question the team wasn't able to answer, do you reach out to the property team to get that information, or one of us?
No, so the Q&A that we're doing is… let me skip forward to it.
It is… we are seating and answering these questions, so our tool is only responding to the questions that we're posting.
Okay, got it.
So we've compiled some of the most, like, frequently asked Q&A questions, and also want to make them as SEO-friendly as possible by highlighting amenities.
So… only highlighting amenities that your property does have, so you may see these questions different property by property.
